findings.recommendation: Apply 'rules not stories' principle: remove Philosophy (3 lines), Safety Features (20 lines), Quality Standards (16 lines), Helper Scripts Used (7 lines), Usage (11 lines). These sections provide explanatory context that belongs in /dev.md, not command execution documentation. All violations are pre-existing — none introduced by this diff (diff changed only line 590: '/commit then /push' -> '/push'). Target reduction: 57 lines (7.5% -> ~3%). Reference: convert.md cleanup (commit 2d21631) reduced 113 lines (-22%) by removing similar verbose sections.

diff_analysis.change_description: Replaced '/commit (to register the commit and write the push token), then /push' with '/push' in Step 17 Next Steps

clean_files.note: No verbose section patterns detected. All sections are functional (Your Role, Input Format, Execution Guidelines, Output Format, Safety Rules, Checkpoint Marking Contract). The 6 lines added in this diff (push-gate token writing procedure under '## Git Operations') are procedural instructions, not verbose fluff.
